title: Flood extent mapping for road network closure decisions
summary: Satellite flood mapping gives road operators a network-wide inundation picture for closure decisions.
problem: During major floods, road operators must make closure decisions across hundreds of kilometres at once. Road-weather sensors may be submerged or offline, and incident reports are too slow and localised to give an accurate network-wide picture.
solution: CEMS Rapid Mapping provides Sentinel-1-derived flood extent maps within hours, updated through the event and integrated into GIS-based traffic-management systems. Operators gain a comprehensive, accurate basis for closure and reopening decisions.

copernicusRole.detail: CEMS Rapid Mapping flood-extent products are loaded directly into traffic-management GIS for closure decisions; no user-side processing of the satellite data is required.
